feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Pricing Error in converting exchange rate
entry.content: In my TDD to build a demo of ordering process for a customer, I am facing pricing error per the screen. Since configuration architect says it is only happening to Cty: Japan(JPY).  Please clarify how I go through this error.

entry.title: What is the difference between a review booklet and a report?
entry.content:
Our team is considering switching from query reports to review booklets.
Therefore, we have the following questions, and we would appreciate your guidance:
1. What are the differences between reports and booklets, and how does SAP intend them to be used?
2. How many of the current query reports/which areas of applications are planned to be replaced with review booklets? How long will the transition take?

entry.title: Set Posting Periods Automatically
entry.content:
We are considering the possibility of automating the opening of posting periods. We have noticed that there is an option in the Manage Posting Periods app to do this, but it is not compatible with special periods. In other words, this job may work well for most of the year, when special periods are not open. However, at the end of the year (December), when the finance team opens the special periods to post the adjustments, the job will fail with the following error:

Failed to schedule recurrence because the item contains special periods. Please set posting periods manually or schedule jobs one by one

We therefore understand that this functionality will only work if special periods are not used.

Are we correct? If so, is there any other alternative?

entry.title: Test Guidelines when extending solution using CLT
entry.content:
Team

Are there any defined SAP guidelines or recommendations on how to test when a solution is extended using CLT in S4 HANA public cloud?

 

Regards

Vidhi

entry.title: Application- Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document
entry.content:
Dear SAP support,

We have created 2 BIN's in GR-AREA, (GR AREA-1 & GR-AREA-2) but in Application- Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document, i am not getting BIN field so i can select it manually. screenshot attached.

This is mobile application and i want to do it from mobile only.

Regards,

Saloni Tiwari

entry.title: consider production changeover productivity by taking into account item attributes
entry.content:
In SAP Public Cloud business requirements: When rescheduling in reverse order, we want to consider production changeover productivity by taking into account item attributes (e.g., "lighter color first", "narrower width first", "co-product line item count order", etc.) to perform the rescheduling.

Meaning:

  • Lighter color first: In processes such as dyeing, arranging items from lighter to darker colors can reduce cleaning time inside the mixer. (We want to rearrange the production order by lighter color first, switch the manufacturing version, reduce capacity requirements, and then reschedule the production orders.)

  • Narrower width first: In processes where rewinding is done while cutting the width, suppose there are two orders: one order produces nine 10cm-wide strips from a 100cm-wide original roll (assuming 10cm loss), and another order produces four 20cm-wide strips (assuming 20cm loss). If the 10cm-width order is scheduled before the 20cm-width order, it is only necessary to remove the cutter blades from 10 to 5. However, if the order is reversed, additional blades must be added, resulting in longer changeover time.

  • Co-product line item count order: This requirement is similar to the "narrower width first" logic. In cases where multiple different widths are cut simultaneously to produce several types, we want to arrange the orders in descending order of the co-product line item count + 1 (main item) + 1 (edge loss – assuming 2 edge strips are generated).

    Which scope should be used, and how can I set up master data to achieve this requirement?

entry.title: Check Stock Overview - Report Clarifications
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We have run the Check Stock Overview Report and noticed that the GL Selection option is available. Some Inventory GLs are matching with the Trial Balance, while some GLs are not reflecting correctly.

We would like clarification on how the report running mechanism works, especially regarding:

  • How the system fetches and validates Inventory GL balances
  • The logic behind GL Selection in the report
  • Possible reasons why certain GLs are matching with the Trial Balance while others are not

Kindly provide your clarification on this issue.

entry.title: SAPcloud版本 付款申请功能操作问题
entry.content:
1、采购订单维护付款定金类别、付款比例、到期日时，能不能维护抬头之后，可以增强或者有什么方法自动维护到采购订单行项目中：

2、问题描述：创建付款申请APP提交申请单之后，需要在管理付款申请APP界面，出纳过账付款时，可以选择多个已批准的付款申请单：

3、财务在创建付款策略时，能不能把维护开户行、账户标识、供应商银行类型相关字段放在付款过账的同一个界面，不需要点击导航按钮进入到另一个界面维护，精简用户操作；

 

CB账号：CB9980000013

entry.title: Request to activate scope ID J77
entry.content:
Hi Team,

requesting you to activate scope ID J77 for the Payment approval matrix configuration for Supplier payments through Automatic Payment Program. 

Kindly let us know if you require any further information.

entry.title: Barcode scanning in “Count Physical Inventory” app – S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi experts,
I am using SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud (Fit-to-Standard).
I am performing cycle counting using the standard apps:
- Create Inventory Documents – Cycle Counting
- Count Physical Inventory
I have navigated to the item-level screen in the “Count Physical Inventory” app.
I would like to understand how barcode scanning is supported in this process.
Currently, I do not see any scan button in the app.
My questions:
1. Does the standard app support barcode scanning?
2. If yes, where should the scanning be performed (which field)?
3. Can we use external barcode scanners (keyboard input)?
4. Is custom development (sap.ndc.BarcodeScannerButton) required for camera-based scanning?
Appreciate your guidance. Thank you.

entry.title: Initial Stock Wrong Price Change -> "Allow Open Status for Previous Period" in CKMLCP
entry.content:
In an implementation project in S4HC Public, with actual costing, we have done in month X-1 the initial stock update, quantity * initial price of material master (Inventory Account BSX to Intial Stock Charge Account BSA). Then the client realized that the prices were wrong, so we did an MR21 with the stocked already charged (BSX to Price Difference Account UMB). Later I realized that the correct way should have been reversing the initial stock, changing the price and then post the initial stock again. Now, at the end of month X-1, we have Inventory Account balance against both Price Difference Account and Initial Stock Charge Account (not only Initial Stock Charge Account as it should be). Executing CKMLCP for X-1 clears the Price Difference Account to 0 but Inventory account goes back to initial price (because of PUP), so this is not a solution.
We came up with this only solution:
- Doing a manual posting to clear Price Difference Account against the Initial Stock Charge Account, so that Inventory Account BSX matches Initial Stock Charge Account. Even though we cannot do this posting at the level of material/plant, this will leave accounting with the correct accounts for month X-1 (Initial Stock of month X)
- Not executing CKMLCP for X-1 and executing it for the first month of the go-live (month X) activating the flag "Allow Open Status for Previous Period" in Preparation step Parameters of CKMLCP. This way, the Price Differences are not carried forward to month X (they are cleared with the manual posting mentioned above), the initial stock of month X is the correct one for all materials (with the new price after the MR21) and only the price differences that appear on month X will be used for the month X Material Ledger Closing.

Do you think this approach is the optimal one given the mistake of doing MR21 with stock already charged? Will activating the flag "Allow Open Status for Previous Period" work for Month X Material Ledger Closing? (I have done a test in DEV system and it allowed me to execute with no problem at all with previous opened periods.

entry.title: [i] CHECKING FOR ADDITIONAL PARAMETERS IN CREATE DUNNING NOTICES APP
entry.content:
Hello Team, 

Requesting checking if the Additional Parameters in Create Dunning Notice Application is feasible to add additional parameters. The Parameters that business wants to add is Line of Business. 

Please let me know as soon as possible if this request is feasible to implement in the Create Dunning Notice App. 

Thank you!

Best regards,

Gelo

entry.title: Public Cloud 2602 / Can you define a standard Company Layout for My Home ?
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

i have a question regarding the customizing of the Space "My Home". As far as i know, if permitted, the user is able to customize his own "My Home" Page and hide or add different functions. 
I also know about the App "Launchpad Settings" and the settings that can be made there.

My question is, is it possible to define a standard layout for "My Home" for all users in the system and prohibit them from changing it ? With layout i mean, can the user see certain tiles, the news or  the section "Insights".
If it is, please explain how this can be achieved.

Best regards
Lukas

entry.title: Maintenance Order Actual and Planned Costs fields not available in Custom CDS View
entry.content:
Hello,
We are connecting Power BI to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.
When using I_MaintenanceOrderBasic as Primary Datasource, the fields TotalPlannedCost and TotalActualCost are not available in the Elements tab.
Which CDS View should be used to get Maintenance Order Planned and Actual costs for Power BI reporting via OData ?
Thank you !

entry.title: Custom Field is automatically uppercase
entry.content:
After selecting an item from the dropdown suggestion list (code list), the value displayed in the custom field is automatically converted to uppercase.

However, for some previous custom fields, when I selected a value from the dropdown list, the system kept the original key value instead of converting it to uppercase.

Can someone explain why this happens? Is there any background reason for this behavior, and is there a way to resolve it?

entry.title: MANDATORY TEXT HEADER FIELD AT GROUP JOURNAL ENTRY POST
entry.content:
We would like to implement a validation rule to make mandatory the text field of the header, DocumentItemText, of a group journal entry.

It should be validated when the user would post through the following applications:

  • “Post Group Journal Entries” F2971
  • “Import Group Journal Entries” F3073

Our approach is to create a validation rule through "Manage Substitution/Validation Rules – Group Journal Entries" F4407 app, using "GR Journal Entry Item" as business context.

However, when searching for the field in the Validation section of the rule, we are unable to locate the field "DocumentItemText" in the Field/Function selector.

 

Is “DocumentItemText” the right name of the related text field?

If yes, would you mind to let us know how could it be enabled to make it mandatory?

 

Thank you in advance for your support.

 

Best regards,

INETUM Team

Almudena Va

entry.title: S/4HANA Public Cloud: How to include Freight Charges in MAP without creating an Account Payable?
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

I am currently working on an S/4HANA Public Cloud implementation and I have a specific requirement regarding Freight Charges in Transportation Management (TM) that I haven't been able to solve using standard configuration.

The Scenario: In our Transport Order, we need to include two specific Charge Types:

  1. SF04 - Basic Rate: Standard freight cost.

  2. ZB01 - Fuel to Carrier (Custom): A charge representing fuel provided physically by the company to the carrier.

The Requirement: Both charges must increase the Moving Average Price (MAP) of the materials being transported. The system is currently performing the Accrual Posting correctly, increasing the inventory value.

The Problem: The charge ZB01 (Fuel) should NOT create an open item in Accounts Payable (MIRO).

  • The fuel is provided in-kind from our own stock (consumed via MIGO against a Cost Center), so we do not owe this amount to the carrier.

  • If we include this line in the MIRO, we create an incorrect liability.

  • If we remove the line or set it to zero during MIRO, the system triggers a cost adjustment that reduces the material's MAP, reversing the initial (and correct) accrual.

What we have explored:

  • We looked into making the Charge Type statistical, but in the current Public Cloud version, this setting seems to be global/header-level and not specific per Charge Type (unlike Condition Types in MM).

  • We need the inventory to absorb the cost, but the offsetting entry should ideally go against a G/L account (like a fuel consumption/offset account) instead of the Supplier BP.

Technical Question: Has anyone faced a similar requirement where a Landed Cost needs to affect the material valuation but should be excluded from the Supplier Invoice (MIRO) without reversing the cost in the material master?

Any workarounds, specific Account Key configurations, or insights on how to handle "in-kind" transport charges in Public Cloud would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance for your help!

Best regards,

Sergio Bravo

entry.title: Skipping Invoice Checks for Reversal Events in MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD
entry.content:
I am using MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD for invoice checking.
This method is automatically triggered during posting, checking, and reversing.
How can I distinguish between these events and skip the check for reversing events?

entry.title: S4HC Public: From Cost Collectors to WBS Elements
entry.content:
Hi experts,

We are implementing S4HC Public to a client who produces cement and concrete: 

  • They are currently on SAP R/3 and use cost collectors.

  • They perform periodic allocations and distributions to orders that have an associated cost collector.

  • In S/4HANA Cloud Public, the standard setup does not allow to use the order as a receiver for those allocations; the standard configuration only allow cost centers or WBS elements as receivers.

  • Since they do not use standard costs, routings, MRP, or PP planning, their process is not a classic PP manufacturing setup.

  • Their real process looks more like monthly cost accumulation and periodic settlement.

¿Would it be a good idea to propose to this client to switch from cost collectors to WBS elements? Can WBS elements be periodically settled to a materials?

entry.title: Freight Unit with Non Stock Material
entry.content:
Hello SAP experts,

I am working with SAP Transportation Management (TM) in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and I have a question about a generated Freight Unit with Non Stock Materials.

How can I create a Freight Unit with a non-stock material in SAP S/HANA Cloud Public Edition?

Thank you for your insights!
